title: 8th and9th Angel Confusion
wikitext:
[]: 8th and9th Angel Confusion             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 07, 2008 6:29 am You know what I always thought was weird? In ep 11 after Fuyutsuki commented that it would suck if an Angel were to attack during the power outage. And then ironically right after the UN is saying that the 8th Angel is attacking. Here, let me get the script. Staff: Radar has detected an unidentified object. Projected landing point is in the former Atomi region. Sub Commander B: It must be Angel number eight

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Mon Jan 07, 2008 6:40 am That's because the UN doesn't know about LILITH Also, entering Terminal Dogma is restricted to only the highest members of NERV. Command Ikari, Sub-Commander Fuyutsuki and Ritsuko are the only ones allowed access to the facility. Although I am surprised that the Commanding Generals, which I think are of higher authority than Ikari, aren't allowed this information. And it wasn't a dubbing error either, because of the secrecy of the Second Angel, would cause some confusion to the UN if it was rleased. Because of that, they really are miscalculating the designation for each Angel.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 07, 2008 6:51 am Isn't Lilith a secret to most Nerv personel as well, Misato for example. But everybody is still using the correct numbers.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Jan 07, 2008 9:32 am we think that's a plot point: the Japanese government and military doesn't know about Lilith

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Jan 07, 2008 4:31 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: Now, this may just be the dub.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Jan 07, 2008 4:45 pm actually I think they might not, but then again that might just be an inconsistency...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 07, 2008 7:40 pm Zugzwang wrote: The way to confirm if the JSSDF don't know about Lilith so give the Angels the wrong number thing, is to check the other scenes where they are talking about the Angels and see if they make the same mistake - do they name Sachiel as the 2nd Angel as an example?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Jan 07, 2008 10:22 pm In any case, Misato clearly calls Israfel the seventh Angel in both the dub and sub, and it's a simple matter counting back to see that number two is missing. And no, they never mention the number of the Angels anywhere else. (Both these tidbits imply that the second Angel is a secret.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 07, 2008 10:43 pm UrsusArctos wrote: they never mention the number of the Angels anywhere else.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jan 08, 2008 4:22 am Misato's is the first instance where a number is spoken out aloud (Although if I remember, Shamshel is numbered all the way in Episode 03, onscreen). Other instances? Sixth Angel, Gaghiel and again, Fourth Angel, Shamshel. 12th Angel Leliel...those are on-screen.

[]:             Quote Post by evilhead008 » Tue Jan 08, 2008 8:48 pm Lilith's existence is kept secret to most NERV employees, as well as the UN. Thus, the UN begins counting at Sachiel, the "Second" Angel, or the First Descendant of Adam. They do not know about Lilith, and I doubt they would count her... she hasn't really done anything besides create humanity and all that.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jan 08, 2008 9:07 pm Actually, the wierd thing is that Misato and the bridge operators know perfectly well which Angel is which but they've never wondered why the Second Angel is missing. I wonder what the story was behind the unused number?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 09, 2008 8:31 pm I'm pretty sure this is mentioned somewhere in the Platinum/Renewal booklet Episode 11 Commentary....Too bad Monkey's site is no more. (And I can't check my copy right now 'cause I'm not at home.)
